subject: early-literacy
script: This is the letter B. B makes the buh sound like ball.

subject: math
script: Let us count slowly together. One, two, three.

subject: math
script: This is a circle. It is round like a wheel or a ball.
media_hint: big round moon and ball animation

subject: phonics
script: Cat has the short a sound. Say it with me: c-a-t.

subject: math
script: You had six and then got four more. Six plus four makes ten.

subject: reading
script: Goal is a simple word you can read. Say each sound: g-o-a-l.
media_hint: soccer goal sign with bouncing letters

subject: reading
script: The main idea is what the whole paragraph is mostly about.

subject: math
script: Three groups of four means four plus four plus four. That is twelve.

subject: logic
script: Look for what repeats and choose what comes next in the pattern.
media_hint: space tiles blinking in a repeat pattern

subject: math
script: When the pieces are the same size, the fraction with more pieces shaded is greater.
media_hint: builder tiles split into equal parts

subject: reading
script: Look at the sentence before and after the word to figure out what it most likely means.

subject: world-knowledge
script: Engineers test designs to make sure they are safe, strong, and work the way they should.

subject: early-literacy
script: This is the letter A. A makes the ah sound like apple.
media_hint: big red apple with letter A animation

subject: math
script: This is a triangle. It has three sides and three corners.
media_hint: colorful triangle tent with three corners highlighted

subject: math
script: Let us count slowly together. One, two, three, four, five.
media_hint: five jumping frogs counted one at a time

subject: phonics
script: Bed has the short e sound. Say it with me: b-e-d.
media_hint: sports scoreboard with bed mascot card

subject: math
script: Ten minus four means taking four away from ten. Six are left.

subject: reading
script: Look at each letter. Say the sound. Put them together to read the word.
media_hint: sports card with letter tiles building a short word

subject: reading
script: A cause is what made something happen. An effect is what happened because of it.
media_hint: rocket launch timeline with cause and effect arrows

subject: math
script: Line up the ones, tens, and hundreds. Add each column from right to left.

subject: math
script: The tenths place is the first digit after the decimal point. The hundredths place is the second.
media_hint: building blueprint with decimal grid overlay

subject: reading
script: Text evidence is a detail from the passage that proves your answer. Look for exact words.
media_hint: construction blueprint with highlighted evidence lines

subject: math
script: Count each picture one time. Point as you count: one, two, three.

subject: math
script: Count all five items slowly. Keep your finger moving one by one.

subject: math
script: A circle is round and has no corners. It looks like a wheel or a ball.

subject: math
script: A triangle has three sides and three corners. Look for the pointed shape.

subject: phonics
script: Short a sounds like apple and cat. Listen for the open ah sound.

subject: math
script: Find the two parts that make ten. You can count up from the bigger number.

subject: math
script: Ten minus something means taking that many away from ten. Count what is left.

subject: phonics
script: Short e sounds like bed and hen. Listen for the eh sound in the middle.

subject: reading
script: The main idea is what the whole passage is mostly about, not one tiny detail.

subject: math
script: Three groups of four mean four plus four plus four. That makes twelve.

subject: math
script: Line up the hundreds, tens, and ones. Add carefully and regroup when needed.

subject: reading
script: A cause makes something happen. Ask what happened first and what changed next.

subject: math
script: When pieces are the same size, the fraction with more shaded pieces is greater.

subject: math
script: The digit after the decimal starts in the tenths place. The second digit is the hundredths place.

subject: reading
script: Good evidence is a detail from the passage that directly proves the answer.

subject: reading
script: Use the nearby words in the sentence to narrow the meaning of the tricky word.
